title: stachys cretica subsp. mersinaea
intro: Stachys cretica subsp. mersinaea, also known as Mersin betony, is a plant species in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to the Eastern Mediterranean region. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Mersin betony include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from digestive problems, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea, used in infusions, or applied topically to the affected area in the form of a cream or ointment.
